As of late October 2025, the World Health Organization has not declared any disease a pandemic. While there are ongoing public health emergencies and concerns—such as Mpox (which was reaffirmed as a Public Health Emergency of International Concern in mid-2025) and H5N1 (which saw an isolated human death in the US in January 2025 without sustained human-to-human transmission)—none of these are currently exhibiting the multi-region, widespread human-to-human transmission that would necessitate a formal "pandemic" characterization. 

Furthermore, the WHO is generally conservative with the term, having only used it a few times in modern history (e.g., H1N1 in 2009, COVID-19 in 2020). With about two months left in 2025, there is no active outbreak on a global exponential trajectory that could realistically prompt a sudden pandemic declaration before December 31. Even if a novel virus were discovered today, it would likely take more than two months for the WHO to escalate its classification to "pandemic," as seen with COVID-19.

The Polymarket price of 0.04 (4%) is higher than the empirical base rate for a 2-month window (<<1%), but this is typical for prediction markets where extreme tail risks often exhibit long-shot bias. Thus, I estimate the true probability at the minimum allowed bound of 2%.
